In that moment, a chain reaction of events occurs. The impacting asteroid exerts so much pressure on the Earth that soil and rock flow like fluids. These glass-like chunks, called tektites, some as large as buses but most the size of marbles, pelt the Earth at speeds ranging from 160 to 320 kilometers per hour in lethal quantities.
